findings.section: SECTION 3: SCAFFOLD & INFRASTRUCTURE (Phase 1) → FC-001
findings.issue: Pre-build checklist references undefined scaffold repository URL and provides no mechanism for the founder to obtain the verified scaffold that Phase 7 template requires to exist.
findings.evidence: FC-001 Step 1 says "Clone the verified scaffold repository (Phase 7 provides the scaffold template)" but does not specify the actual repository URL, branch, or how/where the founder accesses it. Phase 7 template (phase_context_from_prior_phases) states: "The verified scaffold (D-58) is the foundation of every PlatformForge build" and "Phase 7 must produce...verified scaffold location and access instructions."
findings.recommendation: Add explicit step: "Access the verified scaffold at [EXACT GITHUB URL]. Clone: git clone [URL] . If you don't have access, request onboarding from Phase 7 team at [CONTACT]." Include scaffold version number and verification checksum.

findings.section: SECTION 2: PRE-BUILD CHECKLIST → Hosting Provider
findings.issue: Hosting provider selection (Railway, Vercel, Render) is listed as "must exist before card FC-001" but Phase 5 deliverable selection of which provider to use is not referenced or summarized, leaving the founder without guidance on which one to choose.
findings.evidence: Pre-Build Checklist says "Hosting provider account created (e.g., Railway, Vercel, Render — as selected in Phase 5)" but does not provide the Phase 5 decision or selection criteria. Founder has no basis to choose between three options.
findings.recommendation: Add subsection "HOSTING PROVIDER SELECTION" that references D5 (Phase 5 Cost Projection Model section) for the decision already made, and explicitly state which provider was selected for Ocean Golf MVP (e.g., "Phase 5 selected Railway for cost efficiency and GitHub integration"). If decision is deferred, state so explicitly.

findings.section: SECTION 5: FEATURE VERTICAL SEQUENCING — MVP TIER (Area 6 of template)
findings.issue: Area 6 of the phase template requires "Reference D3 complexity for card decomposition" but the deliverable provides card FC-024 through FC-047 (24 cards for Core Booking Management) with no explicit reference to D3 Feature Registry complexity ratings or justification for the card count per feature.
findings.evidence: Template says: "When breaking features into build cards, use the Phase 3 Feature Registry's complexity rating as a guide for card count: Low complexity features → 1-3 cards, Medium → 3-6 cards, High → 8-16 cards, Very High → 20-40 cards." D8 Section 5 says "24 cards represent the complete booking triage workflow" but does not state which features these 24 cards implement or cite D3 complexity ratings.
findings.recommendation: Add table mapping each of the 24 booking cards (FC-024 through FC-047) to D3 feature ID (e.g., F-HUNT-001, F-ACTION-001), show the complexity rating from D3, and explain why that complexity warrants the card count selected. For example: "F-HUNT-002 (High complexity, urgency scoring algorithm) → 3 cards (FC-026, FC-027, FC-046) because scoring logic is core to hunt list priority but can be broken into: algorithm implementation, UI display, real-time recalculation."

findings.section: SECTION 6: BUILD TIMELINE & SPRINT STRUCTURE → Card Dependency Graph
findings.issue: The sprint calendar (Sprint 1, 2, 3) specifies card numbers and names (FC-001 through FC-047 in Sprints 1-3) but does not provide the critical dependency graph required by the phase template: "every card specifies the correct AI tool" and shows which cards block which others.
findings.evidence: Template requires: "a complete, ordered build sequence...dependencies in every card...critical path identification." The sprint calendar shows timeline but not dependencies. For example, FC-046 (real-time updates) likely depends on FC-013-017 (verification infrastructure), but this is not explicit.
findings.recommendation: After the sprint timeline, add "DEPENDENCY GRAPH" section showing (1) which cards are blocking dependencies for later cards, (2) which cards can run in parallel, (3) critical path (longest dependency chain that determines minimum build time). Format: "FC-024 Hunt List Page depends on: FC-004 (users table exists), FC-007 (bookings table exists), FC-018 (auth middleware available). Can start: Day 5 (after FC-018 completes)."

findings.section: SECTION 5: SCAFFOLD & INFRASTRUCTURE (Phase 1) → FC-002
findings.issue: FC-002 card (Design token application) references "design tokens from Phase 6 to the scaffold's shell layout — primary color, font family, sidebar/header dimensions" but does not specify exact hex values, font names, or dimensions, relying on "Phase 6 design tokens" that are not provided in this deliverable.
findings.evidence: Step 2 in FC-002 says "Configure Tailwind design tokens" and references D7 "Section 1.3: Design Tokens" and a tailwind.config.ts example with placeholder colors (#FFFFFF, #111827, etc.) but does not state whether these are the actual Ocean Golf tokens or examples. Founder reading D8 alone cannot execute FC-002 without D7.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit reference section at top of FC-002: "This card requires D7 (UI/UX Decision Record) Section 1.3 for exact color hex values, typography scale, and spacing tokens. Load D7 before executing FC-002. If D7 is unavailable, pause at FC-002 and request from Phase 6 team." Alternatively, reproduce the exact token values in D8 FC-002 (copy from D7) so the card is self-contained.

findings.section: SECTION 3: SCAFFOLD & INFRASTRUCTURE → FC-001 Pre-Build Verification
findings.issue: The pre-build verification section omits checking that GitHub Actions is enabled on the repository and that GitHub Secrets (for environment variables) can be created, which are prerequisites for the CI/CD pipeline card (FC-003) to function.
findings.evidence: Template requires "Pre-Build Checklist: every account, credential, and configuration that must exist before FC-001." GitHub Actions and Secrets setup are infrastructure prerequisites but are not listed. FC-003 assumes these are available.
findings.recommendation: Add to "GitHub" section of Pre-Build Checklist: "[ ] GitHub Actions enabled in repository settings (Admin → Actions → Enable). [ ] GitHub Secrets created for: SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY (navigate to Settings → Secrets and Variables → Actions → New repository secret)."

findings.section: SECTION 2: PRE-BUILD CHECKLIST → Email Service Selection
findings.issue: Email service selection (Resend vs. SendGrid) is listed as required pre-build account but Phase 5 does not specify which one was selected for Ocean Golf, leaving the founder uncertain which one to set up.
findings.evidence: Checklist says "Email Service (for notifications): [ ] Resend account created (https://resend.com) OR SendGrid account created (https://sendgrid.com)" without stating Ocean Golf's choice. D5 would contain this decision, but it's not referenced.
findings.recommendation: State explicitly: "For Ocean Golf, Phase 5 selected [RESEND | SENDGRID] because [REASON: e.g., 'Resend is simpler for startup scale and integrates with Node.js']. Set up account at [EXACT URL]." If deferred to Phase 7, state "Email service selection deferred to Phase 7 card FC-075."

findings.section: SECTION 7: BUILD DECISION RECORDS (D-55 Running Ledger)
findings.issue: The D-55 ledger in Section 7 contains 19 entries (D55-P7-001 through D55-P7-019) but the template requires these decisions to be "Binds" that show downstream work dependent on each decision. The ledger does not show which deliverables or subsequent phases must respect each decision.
findings.evidence: Template example shows: "Binds — what downstream work must respect this (e.g., 'Phase 8 D9 credential guide uses this sequence as its skeleton; D8 card dependencies encode this order')." The D8 ledger lists decisions but not their downstream bindings.
findings.recommendation: Add "Binds" column to D-55 ledger table showing which deliverable or phase must respect each decision. Example: "D55-P7-001 (Auth vertical before features) — Binds: D9 (Phase 8) credential guide skeleton; D8 (Phase 7) card dependencies; Phase 7 implementation order locked to this sequence."

findings.section: SECTION 6: BUILD TIMELINE & SPRINT STRUCTURE → Sprint-Level Build Calendar
findings.issue: The sprint calendar shows dates and card ranges but does not show which days are testing/verification gates vs. active build days, making it unclear when the founder should be available for testing (per template requirement: "Verification gates between card groups").
findings.evidence: Template specifies: "Define three types of verification: Card-level, Integration, Milestone." The calendar lists Checkpoint 1, 2, etc., but does not show which days/times the founder needs to be present to test (Thursday morning sessions are listed separately). Merged calendar would be clearer.
findings.recommendation: Add legend to sprint calendar showing: "Build days (Mon-Wed, dev team works): FC-001, FC-002, etc. | Founder testing day (Thu morning, 10:00-11:30 AM): Verify checkpoint criteria | Friday (cleanup + prep): Final verification, git commit, prep for next week." This aligns the calendar with the separate "Founder Testing Schedule" table.

findings.section: SECTION 4: DATABASE ARCHITECTURE (Phase 2) → FC-004 through FC-012
findings.issue: Database migration cards (FC-004 through FC-012) specify SQL but do not provide rollback SQL (down-migration) for each card, which is required by the phase template for "independently testable" cards that can be recovered if they fail.
findings.evidence: Template requires: "A card is recoverable when a failure can be undone (via git revert) without breaking anything that was working before." FC-004 user table migration provides CREATE TABLE SQL but no DROP TABLE / rollback instruction. Phase 7 template in Section 3 shows card format includes "Rollback: [For database cards: the down-migration SQL...]"
findings.recommendation: For each database card (FC-004 through FC-012), add explicit "Rollback" field: "If this migration fails after partial execution, run: `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS users CASCADE;` to restore to pre-migration state." Wrap in transaction with BEGIN...COMMIT for safety. Provide exact SQL that reverses the migration's changes.

findings.section: SECTION 5: BUILD CARDS CONTINUED (Condensed Reference) → FC-008 through FC-120
findings.issue: Cards FC-008 through FC-120 are specified in summary form ("FC-008: Create Booking_Courses junction table") but lack the detailed step-by-step, acceptance criteria, and error handling that cards FC-001 through FC-007 contain. This inconsistency violates the principle of "every card is self-contained."
findings.evidence: FC-001 through FC-007 have 15-20 line specifications with steps, acceptance criteria, git commit, and error scenarios. FC-008 onwards are 1-2 line bullets. Template requires: "every build card must be self-contained" and "a non-technical founder can verify it worked by following specific, observable acceptance criteria."
findings.recommendation: Expand cards FC-008 through FC-120 to full specification format (matching FC-001 template) OR explicitly state in the header: "Cards FC-008 through FC-120 are specified in summary form in Section 5 due to length constraints. Full specification for each card will be generated during Phase 7 Card Specification Review (separate document). For now, card titles and dependencies are locked; details TBD." If full specs cannot fit in D8, reference a separate "D8-EXTENDED: Complete Card Specifications" document.

findings.section: SECTION 10: BUILD PLAN REVIEW WITH FOUNDER (Area 10 of template)
findings.issue: Area 10 of the phase template requires "Build Plan Review with Founder" as a conversation area, but D8 does not contain a sign-off or approval section showing that the founder has reviewed and approved the build sequence, dependencies, timing, and risks.
findings.evidence: Template says: "Area 10 — Build Plan Review with Founder 🤝: Present the complete build plan as a narrative...Confirm the founder understands...Identify any remaining decisions or clarifications needed before the build can start." D8 has no "FOUNDER REVIEW" or "APPROVAL" section showing this conversation occurred.
findings.recommendation: Add new section before COMPLETION GATE: "SECTION 11: FOUNDER REVIEW & APPROVAL — [Date, Time, Attendees]. Founder (Rafael) reviewed: [list sections reviewed]. Founder confirmed understanding of: [error recovery protocol, git rollback, tool routing, testing schedule, timeline, dependencies]. Founder approved: [yes/no for each major decision]. Remaining questions/decisions: [list any open items]. Sign-off: [Founder name, date, timestamp]." Currently missing founder sign-off for the build plan itself (distinct from D7 sign-off).

findings.section: SECTION 2: PRE-BUILD CHECKLIST → Complete List Validation
findings.issue: Pre-Build Checklist references 5 external services (Supabase, Stripe, Hosting, Email, GitHub) but the D3 Feature Registry and D5 Service Integration Matrix may specify additional services (e.g., SMS/WhatsApp for reminders, monitoring tools, etc.) that are missing from the checklist.
findings.evidence: D5 Phase 5 would contain Service Integration Matrix listing all external dependencies. D8 references Section 1.3 feature list and states services "as selected in Phase 5" but does not enumerate all of them. FC-033 (WhatsApp message API integration) suggests another service, but no WhatsApp API setup is in pre-build checklist.
findings.recommendation: Before finalizing pre-build checklist, load D5 Service Integration Matrix and enumerate ALL services that must have accounts/credentials before Phase 7 build starts. Add them to checklist with account creation URLs, required API keys, and which card uses them (e.g., "WhatsApp API for FC-033 - created at [URL], API key saved to .env.local as WHATSAPP_API_KEY"). Checklist should be complete and comprehensive, not partial.

findings.section: SECTION 8: ERROR RECOVERY REFERENCE (Area 8 of template)
findings.issue: The error recovery protocol specifies three attempts (retry, simplify, different approach, then block) but does not provide concrete examples specific to Ocean Golf's build, making it hard for the founder to understand when/how to apply this to actual failures.
findings.evidence: Section 8 describes "Step 1: Re-run with context..." but does not show: "Example: FC-032 Send Reminder modal fails with 'Heroicons not found' error. Step 1 retry: Run `npm install @heroicons/react` and re-run. If fails again, Step 2: Break FC-032 into two cards (UI only, then integration)."
findings.recommendation: Add "CONCRETE EXAMPLE: Error Recovery in Action" subsection showing a realistic failure scenario (e.g., database migration timeout, API integration error, component rendering issue) and walking through all three recovery steps for that specific scenario. This makes the protocol concrete and applicable to actual build problems.

findings.section: FINAL COMPLETION GATE → Verification
findings.issue: The completion gate lists 38 checkboxes (all marked complete) but does not specify whether this gate was actually verified by an independent reviewer or is self-assessed by the D8 author. Template requires "independent verification" of gate criteria.
findings.evidence: Template says: "All of the following must be satisfied before Phase 7 is complete. You enforce this strictly." But there is no note stating who verified, when, and whether any criteria failed and were remediated before final approval.
findings.recommendation: Add header to completion gate: "GATE VERIFICATION: Reviewed by [Name/Role] on [Date]. Status: [All criteria passed | N criteria failed, remediated, re-verified]. Sign-off: [Reviewer name, timestamp]." This provides auditability and shows the gate was not just self-checked but independently validated.

findings.section: PHASE OUTPUTS → Partial Deliverable 9 Input Section
findings.issue: D8 states it produces "Partial Deliverable 9 Input: Credential Sequencing Map" but does not provide the actual credential sequencing data structure (which services pre-build, during-build, post-build) for D9 to consume, only describes what D9 will contain.
findings.evidence: Section says: "This is not the full Deliverable 9 — Phase 8 adds the intern-level step-by-step instructions..." but does not actually provide the structured credential sequencing data. D9 Phase 8 author will need to extract/rebuild this from D8 narrative, which violates "structured data handoff" principle.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit "Credential Sequencing Skeleton (For D9)" table in Phase Outputs section: | Service Name | Category (Pre/During/Post) | Setup Card | Environment Variables | Auth Method | Notes |. Fill this table with all services referenced in pre-build checklist, making it a direct input for D9 Phase 8 to elaborate into full guide.

findings.section: SECTION 6: BUILD TIMELINE & SPRINT STRUCTURE → Estimated Build Time
findings.issue: Estimated build time range (240-280 hours, 6-7 weeks) assumes "typical completion rates of 6-8 cards per day" but does not account for the founder's stated availability ("3–4 hours/week feedback time") or explain how solo build work scales to 6-8 cards/day given the founder's limited availability window.
findings.evidence: Section states: "based on Founder's 3–4 hours/week feedback time + non-blocking developer velocity." But if founder is the bottleneck for verification/testing, how can build velocity be 6-8 cards/day when founder only has 3-4 hours available per week? Math doesn't clearly connect.
findings.recommendation: Clarify build velocity assumptions: "Cards are executed by AI coding tool (Cursor IDE or Claude Code CLI), not founder. Founder role during build is verification/testing (30 min per testing checkpoint) + decision-making on edge cases (ad-hoc, asynchronous). Build velocity of 6-8 cards/day assumes AI tool execution time (15-90 min per card) + founder async feedback, NOT founder active work. Founder time commitment is testing Thursdays 10:00-11:30 AM + spot-check verification (5-10 min per week)."

findings.section: SECTION 9: PRE-LAUNCH CONFIGURATION CARDS (Area 9 of template)
findings.issue: Pre-launch cards (FC-105 through FC-120) are mentioned and their titles listed, but no detailed card specifications are provided (steps, acceptance criteria, error handling) unlike the fully specified cards FC-001 through FC-047 and the detailed outline for FC-048 onwards.
findings.evidence: Section 9 lists card titles and milestones but provides no step-by-step instructions for these critical final cards. Template requires: "every build card must be self-contained" with "specific, observable acceptance criteria." These cards (production env setup, domain config, final verification) deserve equal detail to earlier cards.
findings.recommendation: Expand "SECTION 9 — Pre-Launch Configuration Cards" to provide full card specifications for FC-105 through FC-120 (following the same format as FC-001 through FC-007), OR explicitly note: "Pre-launch cards FC-105 through FC-120 require full specification in D8-EXTENDED. Titles and dependencies are locked; detailed steps TBD in Phase 7 week 6."

findings.section: CROSS-REFERENCE MANIFEST: D8 — Exports Section
findings.issue: The cross-reference manifest lists build card exports but does not provide the structured `build_cards` JSON/YAML that downstream tools (Phase 8, Phase 9, project management systems) need to consume programmatically. It only provides narrative descriptions.
findings.evidence: Manifest shows: "`build_cards`: List of card IDs with feature mapping..." but does not actually provide the list in machine-readable format. Phase 7 developers and Phase 8/9 teams will need to manually extract card IDs, dependencies, feature mappings from narrative text.
findings.recommendation: Provide structured data export at end of Phase Outputs section. Format as JSON or YAML: `[{ "card_id": "FC-001", "phase": 1, "tool": "Cursor IDE", "dependencies": [], "feature_ids": [], "estimated_hours": 0.5, "category": "Scaffold" }, { "card_id": "FC-002", "phase": 1, "tool": "Cursor IDE", "dependencies": ["FC-001"], ... }]`. This enables automated phase tracking, dependency validation, and handoff to downstream teams.

findings.section: SECTION 6: BUILD TIMELINE & SPRINT STRUCTURE → Milestone Verification Details
findings.issue: The sprint calendar identifies 12 checkpoints (Checkpoint 1, 2, 3, etc.) but the "SECTION 7: VERIFICATION GATES & ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA" provides detailed milestones only for phases 1-5 (Scaffold, Database, Verification, Auth, Core Booking) but not for phases 6-10 (Clients/Courses, Payment, Notifications, Dashboard, Pre-Launch).
findings.evidence: Section 7 shows "After Phase 5 (Core Booking Management): COMPLETE WORKFLOW TEST" but skips detailed milestone verification for phases 6-9. Template requires: "Milestone Verification after each major phase...production verification at end."
findings.recommendation: Expand Section 7 to include milestone verification checklists for ALL 10 build phases (Phases 6, 7, 8, 9, 10), not just 1-5. Format: "After Phase 6 (Client/Course Databases): [Specific checklist]" and so on. This ensures each phase has clear definition of "done" and founder knows what to verify.
